text: We'll use PostgreSQL as the primary datastore.
label: DECISION

text: Primary keys are UUIDs generated with uuid4().
label: DECISION

text: The default model alias resolves to the mid-tier model, not the flagship.
label: DECISION

text: Task assignment uses round-robin across the available workers.
label: DECISION

text: Routing picks the lowest-latency healthy deployment first.
label: DECISION

text: We select the primary deployment by priority order, then fall back.
label: DECISION

text: Fall back from the primary group to the cheaper group, then to the local one.
label: DECISION

text: The fallback chain goes frontier, then cheap, then local.
label: DECISION

text: Retries use up to 3 attempts with exponential backoff and jitter.
label: DECISION

text: We cap retries at 2 attempts per deployment, then advance to the next.
label: DECISION

text: Clients authenticate with a bearer token in the Authorization header.
label: DECISION

text: Callers send an API key in the X-API-Key header to authenticate.
label: DECISION

text: We'll use shadcn/ui for the component library.
label: DECISION

text: Background jobs run on a Postgres-backed outbox with a polling worker.
label: DECISION

text: The cache backend is Redis with a one-hour TTL.
label: DECISION

text: Streaming is delivered to clients over server-sent events.
label: DECISION

text: The tool registry exposes a register() method that takes a ToolSpec.
label: DECISION

text: Result objects carry a value, citations, and usage fields.
label: DECISION

text: We standardize on uv for packaging across the workspace.
label: DECISION

text: Tracing uses the OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions.
label: DECISION

text: Idempotency keys live in Redis, shared across all instances.
label: DECISION

text: Virtual keys are stored as a SHA-256 hash of the raw token.
label: DECISION

text: The default password hasher is argon2id.
label: DECISION

text: We'll version the API under the /api/v1 prefix.
label: DECISION

text: Config is a single YAML file validated by pydantic at startup.
label: DECISION

text: Money is stored as an integer count of minor units in a BIGINT column.
label: DECISION

text: Server state for the frontend goes through TanStack Query.
label: DECISION

text: The worker looks tools up by name from the shared registry.
label: DECISION

text: Schema migrations are managed with Alembic.
label: DECISION

text: We embed prompts and match cache hits on cosine similarity above 0.97.
label: DECISION

text: Event IDs are UUIDv7 so they sort by creation time.
label: DECISION

text: The dispatcher marks an outbox row as sent in the same transaction.
label: DECISION

text: Provider keys are read from the environment at call time.
label: DECISION

text: We prefer Anthropic over OpenAI within each tier for predictability.
label: DECISION

text: The login endpoint issues a short-lived JWT signed with HS256.
label: DECISION

text: Secrets must never be written to logs under any log level.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Provider API keys must never be stored in the database.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: The JWT secret must only come from the TASKFLOW_JWT_SECRET environment variable.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Every upstream call must have a hard timeout; there is no unbounded option.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: All database access in request handlers must be async; no blocking calls.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Side effects must be emitted exactly once through the outbox, never inline.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Inbound webhooks must be deduplicated by their idempotency key.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: After the retry budget is exhausted the event must go to the dead-letter table, never dropped.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Money must be stored as integer minor units, never as a float.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Rate-limit counters must live in Redis so they survive multiple instances.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: One provider's failure must never be able to crash the gateway.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: OpenAI wire compatibility is non-negotiable; responses must be byte-for-byte correct.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Tools must raise typed ToolError subclasses, never a bare exception.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: The retry policy lives in core; consumers must import it, never reimplement it.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Bad configuration must be a fatal error at startup.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Migrations must go through Alembic; no raw DDL in application code.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: PII must never appear in structured logs.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: All routes must be mounted under the versioned prefix.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: The hard timeout ceiling is a code constant operators cannot override.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Every handler and every failure path must have a test.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: The raw virtual key must never be stored or logged.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Production must use PostgreSQL, never SQLite.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Step handlers must be pure and idempotent.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: The LLM enrichment step must never be able to hard-fail the pipeline.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Upstream credentials must never be forwarded in client-facing headers.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Connection strings must be logged only as [REDACTED].
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Timestamps must be stored in UTC as timestamptz.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: A cache must never be written for a request that failed upstream.
label: CONSTRAINT_HARD

text: Prefer snake_case for database columns and camelCase for JSON fields.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: JSON response fields should follow our camelCase convention.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: We generally keep handlers under a hundred lines for readability.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Prefer keyword arguments for any function with more than three parameters.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Log lines should include the event_id for correlation.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Tool names should be lowercase with underscores.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: The usage ledger is append-only; rows are not updated in place.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Billing should record the model actually served, not the alias.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Pagination defaults to fifty items per page.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: The health endpoint is public and reports provider reachability.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: We prefer dependency injection over module-level singletons.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Public functions should carry docstrings.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: A 504 response should not include the upstream error body.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Local-tier deployments are not cached since they are free.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Status transitions should go through an explicit enum.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: The encryption key is a deployment secret and does not rotate without a redeploy.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Honor the Retry-After header on a 429, capped at eight seconds.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Tests should cover the duplicate-delivery path, not just the happy path.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: The cache key is computed from the resolved model, not the alias.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Use TypeScript strict mode across the frontend.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Keep the dependency surface lean for the gateway.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Group routes by domain rather than by HTTP verb.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: Cache entries expire after an hour to avoid serving stale completions.
label: CONSTRAINT_SOFT

text: We evaluated Celery but rejected it because it requires a Redis or RabbitMQ broker.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: Celery is ruled out; the broker overhead is too much for a local-dev setup.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: We will not use LangChain in the request path; it adds latency and hides control.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: LangChain's tool abstraction is rejected in favor of our typed ToolSpec.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: In-process rate limiting is rejected because counters don't survive multiple instances.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: We rejected sticky sessions for rate limiting; they break on instance restart.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: Inline synchronous side effects are abandoned; they give no exactly-once guarantee.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: Do not revisit MUI or Chakra; we deliberately chose a headless library instead.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: bcrypt is abandoned because it silently truncates passwords at 72 bytes.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: We will not store money as a float; that approach is permanently off the table.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: Round-robin routing was rejected because the deployments are not interchangeable.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: SQLite for local dev is rejected; we standardized on Postgres everywhere.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: Per-package retry logic is forbidden; that duplication was explicitly abandoned.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: Gossip-based counter sync is ruled out; eventual consistency always overshoots.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: We tried budget sharding and dropped it; it can't handle autoscaling.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY

text: Polling the provider with no timeout is a non-starter and will not be revisited.
label: APPROACH_ABANDONED_DO_NOT_RETRY
